# ADR-0054: UI testability contract

**Status**: Proposed (2026-06-22)
**Author**: ObjectUI app-shell / components / fields / plugin-view team
**Consumers**: `@object-ui/app-shell`, `@object-ui/components`, `@object-ui/fields`, every plugin that renders an overlay or input, `@object-ui/cli` (`check`/lint ratchet), and every app generated by the platform
**Evidence**: [audit 2026-06 — UI testability anti-patterns](../audits/2026-06-ui-testability.md)

---

## TL;DR

Architecture decision for a metadata-driven, **AI-authored** app platform whose apps are
increasingly **verified by AI browser testing**. Today a UI can be perfectly usable by a
human yet non-deterministic or unreachable for a machine: a button that re-emits `⌘K`
instead of opening the palette, an overlay you can only `toggle`, state that lives only in
`useState`, primitives with no stable locator, and no global "is the app idle?" signal.

The audit proves this is **a family, not a one-off** (2 synthetic-keyboard triggers, 5
toggle-as-open paths, 56 keyboard handlers, ~40% of clickables with no locator, **0**
global settle signals).

**Decision:** make UI testability a **property of the renderer/design-system**, governed
by a small contract of invariants. Because the platform *generates* UIs, we fix the
shared primitives once and every generated app inherits a testable surface — then a
**CI ratchet** prevents new violations. Testability is treated as **correct-by-construction**,
the same way [ADR-0053] made conflicting list navigation untypable.

### The contract (seven invariants)

| # | Invariant | What it means in code |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C1 | **Idempotent, direct triggers** | Open paths call `open()`, never `toggle()`; `onClick` calls the real handler, never `dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent/MouseEvent)`. |
| C2 | **No sole keyboard/hover/focus gate** | Every action has a click- and/or route-reachable entry; shortcuts and hover are *accelerators only*. |
| C3 | **URL-addressable state** | Open overlay / active filter / wizard step is reflectable in the URL on surfaces where deep-linking matters. |
| C4 | **Stable locators by construction** | The renderer emits `data-testid` + ARIA role/name derived from metadata (object/field/action), not hand-added per app. |
| C5 | **Machine-readable async state** | Async regions expose `aria-busy`/`data-state`, and app-shell exposes one global in-flight/idle signal. |
| C6 | **Trusted-input honesty** | Controlled/debounced inputs are documented as requiring a real-input driver; their settle is observable via C5. |
| C7 | **Isolatable runs** | An app can be driven from a deterministic deep-link with isolated browser context + seeded data (tooling/runbook, not renderer code). |

---

## Context

Two facts make this urgent and make the *central* fix the right one:

1. **The platform generates the UI.** Apps are authored from metadata; renderers
   (`app-shell`, `components`, `fields`, the plugins) turn that metadata into DOM. So a
   testability defect in a primitive is multiplied across every app, and a testability
   *fix* in a primitive is inherited by every app for free.
2. **The author and the tester are increasingly AI.** An AI author will not hand-write
   `data-testid`s or bespoke waits; an AI tester clicks and navigates but does not reason
   about "this only works if I press a real `⌘K` that the browser hasn't reserved." The
   shortcuts that help a human (toggle, hover, key combos, synthetic re-dispatch) are
   precisely what break a machine.

The [audit](../audits/2026-06-ui-testability.md) enumerates the seven anti-pattern
classes and their counts. This ADR turns the audit's "verdict" lines into enforceable
invariants and a rollout.

## Decision detail

### C1 — Idempotent, direct triggers

- **Ban** `onClick={() => document.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent(...))}` (and the mouse
  equivalent that queries a node and dispatches `click`). The control must call the same
  command the shortcut calls.
- **Ban** `toggle`/`setOpen(p => !p)` as the *open* path. Overlay primitives expose
  `open()`/`close()`/`toggle()`; user-facing "open" affordances and programmatic callers
  use `open()`.
- The command palette is the reference fix: a shared `useCommandPalette()` /
  context-provided `openCommandPalette()` that the header button, the `⌘K` handler, and a
  `?palette=…` deep-link (C3) all call.

### C2 — No sole keyboard/hover/focus gate

- Keyboard shortcuts and `onMouseEnter`-reveals remain, but every action they trigger has
  a visible, clickable, or routable equivalent. `check`/lint flags a *new* overlay whose
  only open path is a key combo or hover.

### C3 — URL-addressable state

- Provide a small router-aware overlay helper (e.g. `useUrlOverlay('palette')`) so the
  open state of palettes, action dialogs, record drawers, and wizard steps is encoded in
  the URL where deep-linking is valuable. This is also a shareability/back-forward UX win.
- Not every transient popover needs a URL; the contract targets *navigable* surfaces
  (palette, record drawer, action dialog, wizard step, active filter set).

### C4 — Stable locators by construction

- The renderer emits `data-testid` (and ensures an ARIA role/name) on the elements it
  generates, derived from metadata. Proposed convention:
  `data-testid="{surface}:{entity}[:{action|field}]"`, e.g.
  `field:account.industry`, `action:account.delete`, `list:account:row:{id}`,
  `overlay:command-palette`.
- Apps inherit locators with zero per-app work; this is the highest-leverage invariant.

### C5 — Machine-readable async state

- `app-shell` exposes one global signal (working name `window.__objectui.idle` /
  `pendingRequests`) incremented/decremented by the data layer around in-flight requests,
  so "wait until settled" is a single predicate.
- Async regions (lists, forms, pickers, dashboards) set `aria-busy`/`data-state` so
  region-level waiting is also possible.

### C6 — Trusted-input honesty

- Document (renderer README + e2e runbook) that controlled/debounced inputs (list search,
  record-picker search, lookup) require a **trusted-input driver** (CDP keystrokes), not
  value-injection — and that their completion is observed via C5. Encodes the lesson from
  `memory: reference_browser_e2e_verification`.

### C7 — Isolatable runs

- A runbook (not renderer code): deterministic deep-link + isolated browser context +
  seeded/resettable data + a deterministic test identity. Captures the operational
  gotchas already learned (shared browser-profile contention, per-port `VITE_SERVER_URL`).

## Phasing (each an independent, browser-verified PR)

> **Rollout status (2026-06):** all five phases shipped — Phase 1 (#1879), Phase 2 (#1882), Phase 3 (#1883), Phase 4 (#1887), Phase 5 (ratchet).

This ADR + the audit land first as docs. Implementation is then incremental — each phase
is shippable and browser-verified on its own:

1. **Reference fix + primitive** — command palette to C1 (idempotent `openCommandPalette()`,
   real handler on the button, drop the synthetic `⌘K` dispatch) and a `data-testid`
   (C4). Proves the pattern end-to-end. Add a `useRecordSearch` unit test.
2. **Overlay primitives** — `Dialog`/`Drawer`/`Sheet`/`CommandPalette`/`Menu` default to
   idempotent `open()`+`data-testid`+`data-state`; optional `useUrlOverlay` (C3).
3. **Settle signal** — global in-flight/idle in app-shell's data layer (C5); standardize
   `aria-busy` on list/form/picker/dashboard async regions.
4. **Locator coverage** — `fields` widgets and `components/ui/*` emit metadata-derived
   `data-testid` (C4); close the ~40% gap at the source.
5. **Ratchet** — ESLint rule(s) (ban synthetic-event triggers + `setOpen(p=>!p)` open
   paths) and a `check`/conformance test that fails on a *new* interactive surface without
   a locator. Same "no new declared-but-unenforced surface" spirit as the spec liveness
   ledger — counts can only go down.

## Clean-build, not migrate

Fixtures and sample apps are disposable; we change the primitives in place rather than
shipping back-compat shims. External apps relying on a banned idiom (synthetic-trigger,
toggle-open) get a `check` error and a codemod hint, consistent with [ADR-0053]'s
clean-build stance.

## Alternatives considered

- **Per-app e2e selectors + waits (status quo).** Rejected: re-derives testability in
  every generated app, exactly what a metadata platform exists to avoid, and an AI author
  won't write them.
- **A `mode`/flag discriminator instead of removing the bad idiom.** Rejected for the
  same reason [ADR-0053] rejected it — duplicates intent and lets the wrong state stay
  representable; bans + correct primitives make violations untypable.
- **Lint-only, no primitive changes.** Rejected: lint stops *new* violations but leaves
  the generated baseline untestable; the leverage is in the shared primitives.

## Consequences

- **Positive:** generated apps become AI-testable by construction; deep-links double as
  shareable URLs; one settle signal replaces ad-hoc waits; the ratchet prevents
  regression. The lessons already paid for (synthetic-input, browser-profile isolation)
  are encoded, not re-learned.
- **Cost:** touches high-traffic primitives (overlays, fields) — each phase must be
  browser-verified. The locator convention (C4) is a one-time naming decision to get right.
- **Non-goal:** this ADR does not mandate a specific test runner or write the e2e suites;
  it makes the *surface* deterministic so any driver (and any agent) can.

[ADR-0053]: 0053-list-view-navigation-modes.md
